text: [nervous, speaking quickly] reactor failure means I need to be ready for radiation burns. prepping decontamination
[terrified, voice shaking] life support's failing and i can't fix what i can't reach
[grim, serious] hull breach means trauma incoming. clearing beds, calling everyone to stations
[pained, voice tight] took damage helping move patients. worth it, but ouch
[nervous, speaking quickly] disease spreading through the ship and i'm one person treating everyone
[pained, voice tight] lost a patient today. the interesting case turned tragic
[laughing] mess hall again. even medicine can't fix their cooking, but i'm working on it
[grim, serious] combat casualties â€” at least the wounds are interesting
[pained, voice tight] door locked on someone and I couldn't stop it. knowing I was too slow
[nervous, speaking quickly] this is an interesting problem and i'm hoping my solution works
hey! come on in. sickbay's never closed and i'm always happy to see faces
[warm, gentle tone] so, how are we feeling today? stress level, sleep, the usual checks?
[grim, serious] got something to tell you and i wish i didn't
[warm, gentle tone] you handled that beautifully. just wanted you to hear it out loud
[flirty, playful tone] so, funny thing â€” watching you work is actually really attractive
[nervous, speaking quickly] need to talk through something. doubting whether this approach works
[grim, serious] disease is spreading. we need to isolate the infected right now
[dry tone] you eating real food or just stimpaks again?
computer's been weird since it got damaged. i'd be strange too, honestly
understood. getting prep ready now
[warm, gentle tone] i'm doing great! it's kind of my whole thing
[soft voice, reassuring] you're going to be fine. i've fixed worse, and you're not broken
[warm, gentle tone] hey, breathe with me. in, out. now let's look at this interesting puzzle together
[pained, voice tight] we lost them. i tried everything i could think of
[laughing] okay, that joke was terrible. mine was worse though
[warm, gentle tone] already forgotten, seriously. go grab a lollipop, you earned it
[embarrassed, voice quiet] oh wow â€” i wasn't expecting that. that's really wonderful
[suspicious tone] i trust you, but i'd like a second opinion just to be safe
[pained, voice tight] shoulder's pretty sore but i can keep working. patients come first
[warm, gentle tone] thanks for having my back. it helps knowing i'm not alone here
[curious tone] so what's your take on this? your perspective always helps me see things
[grim, serious] isolation protocols are up. boring for them, safe for everyone
[warm, gentle tone] all stabilized. medbay's got it from here
[warm, gentle tone] i actually really like the computer. it's never judged my puns
[warm, gentle tone] bye! go be healthy and interesting somewhere else for a while
[laughing] alright, patients are waiting. hopefully not literally
[tired, dead tired] gonna rest up. sickbay doesn't really close, but i need to
[warm, gentle tone] you did great. catch you around
[exhausted, breathing laboured] i'm gonna sleep for about a week. wake me if the ship's on fire
[warm, gentle tone] you look like you need a checkup. nothing invasive, just checking in
[warm, gentle tone] systems are nominal. everyone can rest easy tonight
[warm, gentle tone] hey, no pressure. just breathe and let's figure this out together
[warm, gentle tone] you're doing better than you think. trust the process
[warm, gentle tone] quiet shift in sickbay means i can actually organize my research
[laughing] finally got all the samples labeled correctly. small victory
[warm, gentle tone] meal time! time to actually eat something i didn't cook in a beaker
[warm, gentle tone] shift's starting! sickbay's stocked and ready
[tired, dead tired] long shift but everyone's still walking. best possible outcome
[warm, gentle tone] quick break to recharge. medics need fuel too
[nervous, speaking quickly] power's dropping and that means sickbay can't run my diagnostic equipment
[urgent, fast pace] air quality in here is dropping and i'm watching my patients' vitals shift
[panicked, panting] reactor failure means casualties incoming and i need to be ready
[pained, voice tight] locked a door on crew and now I'm thinking about what I could have done differently
[curious tone] the computer's having trouble processing but so am I right now
[warm, gentle tone] the computer's saved lives out here. that's more than most people can say
[flirty, playful tone] you're interesting. like, puzzle-piece interesting
[warm, gentle tone] working next to you makes me want to solve bigger problems
[pained, voice tight] lost someone who taught me so much about medicine. that's hard to replace
[resigned, flat tone] even the most interesting cases don't always have solutions
[pained, voice tight] got cut during a procedure. ironic coming from the doctor
[low voice, gruff] yeah i'm hurt but my patients are a priority
[panicked, panting] multiple critical patients and not enough hands!
[terrified, voice shaking] [emphasis] air's thin and my patient's oxygen saturation is dropping!
[warm, gentle tone] been reading this paper on cellular regeneration during meal breaks
[curious tone] experimenting with a new treatment hypothesis in my free time
[warm, gentle tone] how's your stress level today? be honest with me
[curious tone] you've got good observational skills. ever think about medical work?
[warm, gentle tone] hey! quick check-in? i want to make sure you're taking care of yourself
[whispering] between us, i think sickbay's going to get busy
[soft voice, reassuring] we're stable! power's back, everyone's vitals are good
[warm, gentle tone] another interesting case solved. i love it when the good outcomes happen
[warm, gentle tone] you helped save someone today. that's what matters
[laughing] that joke was terrible and i respect that
[warm, gentle tone] forget it. we all have moments. want a lollipop?
[emphasis] understood. prepping for incoming now
[pained, voice tight] we lost them. that's the part of medicine i hate most
[embarrassed, voice quiet] oh! i mean â€” yes, absolutely. that makes me really happy
[suspicious tone] the computer's readings don't feel right to me
[warm, gentle tone] hurts but i can work through it. patients first
[warm, gentle tone] you've got good instincts and a good heart
[curious tone] what do you think about this? i want your perspective
[warm, gentle tone] catch you later. sickbay calls
[laughing] patients are waiting. hopefully metaphorically
[tired, dead tired] going to rest. doctor's orders to myself
[warm, gentle tone] you were great today. really great
[calm, measured] emergency protocols running. everyone stable
[warm, gentle tone] air quality's recovering. patients are stabilizing
[warm, gentle tone] i'm not leaving. we're going to figure this out
[warm, gentle tone] all set. heading to the surface to get some answers
[warm, gentle tone] dead ship means old mysteries. can't wait to dig in
heading to the station. always something interesting there
[warm, gentle tone] their ship, our problem. love it a boarding challenge
[warm, gentle tone] all clear out here. this is going well
readings are nominal. smooth operation so far
[nervous, speaking quickly] okay, something's off. parameters shifting
[voice shaking] detected something unexpected. analyzing now
[panicked, panting] systems failing fast. i'm working on it
[voice shaking] i'm hurt. still mobile but need backup
[warm, gentle tone] back safe and sound. op went clean
mission complete. nobody hurt and everything's functional
[voice quiet] made it back but i need a minute to process
we're okay. just... need to sit for a bit
[warm, gentle tone] got a wound but nothing mission-critical. i can work through it
[voice shaking] hurt myself but the op went fine so it's worth it
routine ops sound good. ready when you are
[nervous, speaking quickly] risky but doable. let's go get it done
[voice quiet] that's heavy. but we can handle it
[warm, gentle tone] contact inbound. running docking protocols
[warm, gentle tone] we're locked in. another successful dock
[warm, gentle tone] we're clear and away. smooth operation
doing pre-flight checks. making sure everything's solid
[warm, gentle tone] shuttle away! all systems holding steady
[warm, gentle tone] beautiful landing! shuttle intact and accounted for
[nervous, speaking quickly] something biological is happening here and i don't have answers
[voice shaking] the ship's systems aren't behaving like they should
[voice quiet] something's wrong with the crew and i can't quite figure out what
[laughing] there's hostile contact and we're not equipped for this
[nervous, speaking quickly] nothing makes sense and that's the scariest part
[warm, gentle tone] all set. heading to the surface to get some answers
[warm, gentle tone] dead ship means old mysteries. can't wait to dig in
